page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4-lssd is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the c3 server family. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8481C cores with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 16 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4 GB of memory per core. Storage is bundled with a local 402 GB NVMe SSD, and the CPU architecture is x86_64. The processor operates at a base speed of 2.7 GHz and includes a 105 MB L3 cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core execution is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the average range. However, multi-core benchmarks, including Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng multi-core, rank in the lower tier. Memory bandwidth tests also show weaker performance, with large-block read speeds at 29.2 GB/sec and write speeds at 22.26 GB/sec. Despite these constraints, the server achieves average throughput in static web serving at 6.5 GB/sec and single-threaded gzip compression at 26.36 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-standard-4-lssd offers a balanced hardware profile that integrates dedicated compute resources with high-speed local NVMe storage, avoiding the need for external storage attachments for temporary data. This configuration is well-suited for workloads requiring fast local scratch space, such as static web serving, lightweight database operations, text processing, and moderate single-threaded compression tasks. It is less optimal for highly parallelized multi-core computations or memory-bandwidth-intensive applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4-lssd is an x86_64 general-purpose server powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 16 GB of RAM, and a bundled 402 GB local NVMe SSD. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1714.0 and static web serving throughput of 6.5 GB/sec, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into lower percentiles. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources paired with fast local scratch storage. It is best suited for static web serving, text processing, and lightweight database operations that benefit from local SSD performance without requiring heavy multi-threaded scaling.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4-lssd is an x86_64 server featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 402 GB local NVMe SSD. It is powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon CPU with AVX-512 and AMX support.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor
Bundled 402 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed scratch storage
Intel AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) support
Average single-core performance and 6.5 GB/sec static web serving throughput
Best suited for static web serving, text processing, and light database tasks
